texto: It seemed as if the mountains, wave after wave of them, were like the sea, going outward forever into distance, till, far away, they became engulfed in clouds, and joined—mountain and sky in one. Standing there on top, facing the enormity of the world, I thought of myself as a man. How boundlessly small we are…
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tan Kok Seng: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “It seemed as if the mountains, wave after wave of them, were like the sea, going outward forever …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, literature, malaysia pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de malaysia e memoir.
